This situation was the result of a compromise within an individual company's network unrelated to SolarWinds. That breach enabled attackers to add malicious code to the Orion Platform software instance within the customer's network.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-452221a0323dd358446f3b725226ed9f" class="builder-452221a0323dd358446f3b725226ed9f builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>In November of 2020, Sophos investigated an environment infected with a Ragnar Locker attack in which hundreds of computers were compromised.</li><li>One of the computers compromised in the Ragnar Locker attack was a Windows server hosting the Orion product.</li><li>The threat actor had full administrative access to the Windows server and was able to copy and replace a signed .dll shipped with the Orion Platform software with an unsigned, compromised version.</li><li>This compromised code was not shipped with the Orion Platform software.</li><li>Following the Orion Secure Configuration Guide mitigates the risk of this type of attack.</li></ul></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-5cf42379e9196d7ffea16c38d62a1037 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-5cf42379e9196d7ffea16c38d62a1037" class="builder-5cf42379e9196d7ffea16c38d62a1037 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}</style><div class="builder-blocks min-w-0 props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284" builder-path="component.options.navItems.23.blocks" builder-parent-id="builder-1a7c1c84170760d0fb8acd80ed0e491b"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div id="what-is-supernova" builder-id="builder-77c611c2d22440aa55251c0722b25a02" class="builder-77c611c2d22440aa55251c0722b25a02 builder-block"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-a35f39b94bc162091a67e54a8ac6c3a3 {
    color: var(--color-headline, #111111);
font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal, 400);
font-size: var(--text-headline-m, 2.375rem);
line-height: var(--text-headline-m--line-height, 3rem);
margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4, 16px);
  }   @media (max-width: 639px) {
      .builder-a35f39b94bc162091a67e54a8ac6c3a3 {
    font-size: var(--text-subhead-xl, 1.875rem);
line-height: var(--text-subhead-l--line-height, 2.375rem);
  }
    } </style><div builder-id="builder-a35f39b94bc162091a67e54a8ac6c3a3" class="builder-a35f39b94bc162091a67e54a8ac6c3a3 builder-block"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><h3>What is SUPERNOVA?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-d0e3c384f2458ba0e1495e6425acfce9 {
    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-d0e3c384f2458ba0e1495e6425acfce9" class="builder-d0e3c384f2458ba0e1495e6425acfce9 builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-12aded908258323b5515ac3e50ef24ad" class="builder-12aded908258323b5515ac3e50ef24ad builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 24</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-c3891d403d8c87750441d89ca61a9c04" class="builder-c3891d403d8c87750441d89ca61a9c04 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 1/29/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-541dc778ecc306ba73f313945d6152c6" class="builder-541dc778ecc306ba73f313945d6152c6 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>What is SUPERNOVA?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-07679d9880002d610a8e8c43ae841dbd" class="builder-07679d9880002d610a8e8c43ae841dbd builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Shortly after SUNBURST was announced, third parties and the media publicly reported on a malware, now referred to as SUPERNOVA. Based on our investigation, this malware could be deployed through an exploitation of a vulnerability in the Orion Platform. Like other software companies, we seek to responsibly disclose vulnerabilities in our products to our customers while also mitigating the risk that bad actors seek to exploit those vulnerabilities by releasing updates to our products before we disclose the vulnerabilities.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-092ba4b4c4612aa78ff086954212155d" class="builder-092ba4b4c4612aa78ff086954212155d builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>CISA released a <a href="https://us-cert.cisa.gov/ncas/analysis-reports/ar21-027a" dld="malware analysis report embed" dlt="Glossary Rail" target="_blank">malware analysis report</a> on SUPERNOVA on January 27, 2021. The SUPERNOVA malware consisted of two components. The first was a malicious, unsigned webshell .dll “app_web_logoimagehandler.ashx.b6031896.dll” specifically written to be used on the Orion Platform. The second is the exploitation of a vulnerability in the Orion Platform to enable deployment of the malicious code. The latest updates were designed to remediate this vulnerability in all supported versions of the Orion Platform.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-295168abf63c852c3811da881bff762e" class="builder-295168abf63c852c3811da881bff762e builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We constantly work to enhance the security of our products and to protect our customers and ourselves because hackers and other cybercriminals are always seeking new ways to find and attack their victims. We work closely with our customers to address and remediate any potential concerns, and we encourage all customers to run only supported versions of our products and to upgrade to the latest versions to the get the full benefit of our updates, improvements, and enhancements.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-1b187cbbe0d07283e74cc0bf6b196afd {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-1b187cbbe0d07283e74cc0bf6b196afd" class="builder-1b187cbbe0d07283e74cc0bf6b196afd builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {

It is malware that is separately placed on a server that requires unauthorized access to a customer’s network and is designed to appear to be part of a SolarWinds product.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-765591704eea83e30b1e063ba81b8962 {

data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-0fc72265f2d52f79c62ba3ef22b23601" class="builder-0fc72265f2d52f79c62ba3ef22b23601 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>How can I confirm if I’ve applied either the SUPERNOVA mitigation script or one of the SUPERNOVA security fixes to my Orion server(s)?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-a6ffabfd61472dfeeba2f5b4b95cabcb" class="builder-a6ffabfd61472dfeeba2f5b4b95cabcb builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you’re running Orion Platform version 2018.2, 2018.4, or 2019.2, you can run the script available <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Use-a-script-to-verify-that-the-Supernova-patch-was-applied-to-all-Orion-web-servers?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a> to verify one of the SUPERNOVA Security Fixes (2018.2 HF6 Security Fix, 2018.4 HF3 Security Fix, 2019.2 HF3 Security Fix) for the recent SUPERNOVA <a href="https://www.solarwinds.com/sa-overview/securityadvisory" dld="security vulnerability embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">security vulnerability</a> has been applied to your main Orion server and any additional web servers. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply the respective patch.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-769fa5c36c7397bd8b8aae5f6c560867" class="builder-769fa5c36c7397bd8b8aae5f6c560867 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you used the SUPERNOVA Mitigation Script to address the Supernova vulnerability, use the guidance in the document within that package to confirm the temporary patch. If you reinstall your Orion server, you will need to reapply this script.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-11a6e046682fd00eddbd3459fc3307e8 {

</style><div builder-id="builder-f0cec174c3fc658bca5f56bc23f90a37" class="builder-f0cec174c3fc658bca5f56bc23f90a37 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 28</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-597f29c20fa0b0d4aa026f6f5b2085a8" class="builder-597f29c20fa0b0d4aa026f6f5b2085a8 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 12/18/2020</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-d23355d81d31c63fd44b45e45e7521ce" class="builder-d23355d81d31c63fd44b45e45e7521ce builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>What is SUNBURST?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-5ef1d794fbcbefb883e4dd65e1ac9073" class="builder-5ef1d794fbcbefb883e4dd65e1ac9073 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>SolarWinds was the victim of a cyberattack that inserted a vulnerability (SUNBURST) within our Orion® Platform software builds for versions <strong>2019.4 HF 5</strong>, <strong>2020.2 </strong><u><strong>unpatched</strong></u>, and <strong>2020.2 HF 1</strong>, which, if present and activated, could potentially allow an attacker to compromise the server on which the Orion Platform products run. SUNBURST was very sophisticated supply chain attack, which refers to a disruption in a standard process resulting in a compromised result with a goal of being able to attack subsequent users of the software.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-fa103cc7c8f991bcf97ef8f2fce45fb0" class="builder-fa103cc7c8f991bcf97ef8f2fce45fb0 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Based on our investigation, it appears that the code was intended to be used in a targeted way as its exploitation requires manual intervention. We’ve been advised that the sophistication and nature of SUNBURST indicates that it may have been conducted by an outside nation state, but SolarWinds has not verified the identity of the attacker.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-5858c42f422261bd934087fa99346d65 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-5858c42f422261bd934087fa99346d65" class="builder-5858c42f422261bd934087fa99346d65 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}</style><div class="builder-blocks min-w-0 props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284" builder-path="component.options.navItems.28.blocks" builder-parent-id="builder-1a7c1c84170760d0fb8acd80ed0e491b"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div id="how-extensive-is-the-impact-of-sunburst" builder-id="builder-1dc8c6ab0d9f9f545a07961c7de1147c" class="builder-1dc8c6ab0d9f9f545a07961c7de1147c builder-block"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-651815dada8071cc7c49a8bde286aab6 {
    color: var(--color-headline, #111111);
font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal, 400);
font-size: var(--text-headline-m, 2.375rem);
line-height: var(--text-headline-m--line-height, 3rem);
margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4, 16px);
  }   @media (max-width: 639px) {
      .builder-651815dada8071cc7c49a8bde286aab6 {
    font-size: var(--text-subhead-xl, 1.875rem);
line-height: var(--text-subhead-l--line-height, 2.375rem);
  }
    } </style><div builder-id="builder-651815dada8071cc7c49a8bde286aab6" class="builder-651815dada8071cc7c49a8bde286aab6 builder-block"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><h3>How extensive is the impact of SUNBURST?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-588c2268994d78ef98681473b4c21e83 {
    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-588c2268994d78ef98681473b4c21e83" class="builder-588c2268994d78ef98681473b4c21e83 builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-3fcbc041d4272122012eb3d5fa95a807" class="builder-3fcbc041d4272122012eb3d5fa95a807 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 29</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-71f9cf79c0eee8b0c374b2d874bd9ef7" class="builder-71f9cf79c0eee8b0c374b2d874bd9ef7 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 12/18/2020</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-1500fe7c98798031c57de46375832c5a" class="builder-1500fe7c98798031c57de46375832c5a builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>How extensive is the impact of SUNBURST?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-b54f33d2607aefa96b6654ae8e0532df" class="builder-b54f33d2607aefa96b6654ae8e0532df builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>Based on our investigations to date, which are ongoing, we believe that the SUNBURST vulnerability was inserted within the Orion Platform products and existed in updates released between March and June 2020 (which we call the “relevant period”) as a result of a compromise of the Orion software build system and was not present in the source code repository of the Orion Platform products. SolarWinds has taken steps to remediate the compromise of the Orion software build system and we’re investigating what additional steps, if any, should be taken. <strong>Also, while we are still investigating our non-Orion products, we have not seen any evidence that they are impacted by SUNBURST. </strong>Based on our investigations to date, SolarWinds currently believes that:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-4c6529b81afe8b6354e0cba494e75fda" class="builder-4c6529b81afe8b6354e0cba494e75fda builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>Orion Platform products downloaded, implemented or updated during the relevant period contained the SUNBURST vulnerability;</li><li>Orion Platform products downloaded and implemented <strong>before</strong> the relevant period and not updated during the relevant period did not contain the SUNBURST vulnerability;</li><li>Orion Platform products downloaded and implemented <strong>after </strong>the relevant period did not contain the SUNBURST vulnerability; and</li><li>Previously affected versions of the Orion Platform products that were updated with a build released <strong>after </strong>the relevant period no longer contained the SUNBURST vulnerability; however, the server on which the affected Orion Platform products ran may have been compromised during the period in which the SUNBURST vulnerability existed.</li></ul></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-6c41880123680fa952659e5054039e95 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-6c41880123680fa952659e5054039e95" class="builder-6c41880123680fa952659e5054039e95 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;
}</style><div class="builder-blocks min-w-0 props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284" builder-path="component.options.navItems.29.blocks" builder-parent-id="builder-1a7c1c84170760d0fb8acd80ed0e491b"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div id="what-should-i-do-if-i-believe-my-environment-has-been-compromised-by-the-sunburst-vulnerability" builder-id="builder-58101be325de032fa25533442535197c" class="builder-58101be325de032fa25533442535197c builder-block"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-7a4c98dd8b9f55103ed0cce7561f98dd {
    color: var(--color-headline, #111111);

font-size: var(--text-headline-m, 2.375rem);
line-height: var(--text-headline-m--line-height, 3rem);
margin-bottom: var(--spacing-4, 16px);
  }   @media (max-width: 639px) {
      .builder-7a4c98dd8b9f55103ed0cce7561f98dd {
    font-size: var(--text-subhead-xl, 1.875rem);
line-height: var(--text-subhead-l--line-height, 2.375rem);
  }
    } </style><div builder-id="builder-7a4c98dd8b9f55103ed0cce7561f98dd" class="builder-7a4c98dd8b9f55103ed0cce7561f98dd builder-block"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><h3>What should I do if I believe my environment has been compromised by the SUNBURST vulnerability?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-c9476d81cf0051545715425bb96e7f68 {
    font-size: var(--text-body-m, 1.125rem);
line-height: var(--text-body-m--line-height, 2rem);
color: var(--color-body-primary, #444444);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-c9476d81cf0051545715425bb96e7f68" class="builder-c9476d81cf0051545715425bb96e7f68 builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-9619de468ff7a009036711cb33fbf0fe" class="builder-9619de468ff7a009036711cb33fbf0fe builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 30</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-9c75ebbc0df4ecb019e7fb594d7bc5ac" class="builder-9c75ebbc0df4ecb019e7fb594d7bc5ac builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 4/6/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-f463d493dee2773decfc3ac57e33d68c" class="builder-f463d493dee2773decfc3ac57e33d68c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>What should I do if I believe my environment has been compromised by the SUNBURST vulnerability?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ca4f516621ebd24b8d746bf44632075e" class="builder-ca4f516621ebd24b8d746bf44632075e builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you believe your environment has been compromised by the SUNBURST vulnerability, below are the recommended steps to take:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-1687e3717fded6982caab90eee66f97c" class="builder-1687e3717fded6982caab90eee66f97c builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>First, determine the version of the Orion® Platform products you have installed <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Determine-which-version-of-a-SolarWinds-Orion-product-I-have-installed?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>, and verify which hotfix updates you have installed <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Verify-hotfixes-that-have-been-installed?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-59a0027d1d837664c5fbd3c21dbfb5ee" class="builder-59a0027d1d837664c5fbd3c21dbfb5ee builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>If you <strong>have</strong> already upgraded to <strong>2020.2.5</strong>, additional security fixes and protections unrelated to SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA are included in this version. For more information, review the Release Notes <a href="https://documentation.solarwinds.com/en/Success_Center/orionplatform/content/release_notes/orion_platform_2020-2-5_release_notes.htm" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>, and KB article <a href="https://support.solarwinds.com/SuccessCenter/s/article/Remote-Code-Execution-CVE-Frequently-Asked-Questions?language=en_US" dld="here embed" dlt="Glossary Rail">here</a>.</li><li>If you have upgraded to <strong>2020.2.4</strong> or <strong>2019.4 HF 2</strong>, both the SUNBURST and SUPERNOVA vulnerabilities have been addressed in these versions—and they have also been digitally re-signed by newly obtained digital code-signing certificates.</li><li>If you <strong>have not</strong> already upgraded to <strong>2020.2.5</strong>,<strong> 2020.2.4</strong>, or <strong>2019.4.2</strong>, follow the guidance identified for your current version of your Orion Platform products below to help ensure the security of your environment:</li><ul><li>If you’re currently running Orion Platform version <strong>2019.4 HF 4</strong>, we recommend you upgrade to <strong>2020.2.5</strong>, or <strong>2019.4.2</strong> if you are unable to upgrade to the latest version at this time.</li><li>If you’re currently running Orion Platform version <strong>2019.4 HF 5</strong>, we recommend you rebuild your Orion server/VM for all pollers and install Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.5</strong> or <strong>2019.4.2</strong> if you are unable to upgrade to the latest version at this time.</li><li>If you’re currently running Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.1</strong>, <strong>but have run any of the previous infected versions</strong>, rebuild your Orion server and/or VM for all pollers and install Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.5</strong> on a fresh machine.</li><li>If you are running a version prior to Orion Platform version<strong> 2019.4 HF 4</strong>, we do not believe that your system was compromised with this vulnerability. We still recommend as a best practice that you upgrade to the latest version, Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.5</strong>, to take advantage of the latest security improvements.</li></ul></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-07f45701e903879d4c5c8c2e9b3de881" class="builder-07f45701e903879d4c5c8c2e9b3de881 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>To help protect your system, you should also block DNS access to any of the following:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-b8d8d7d37ba55ab1fdb3f3f7a7771d01" class="builder-b8d8d7d37ba55ab1fdb3f3f7a7771d01 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>avsvmcloud[.]com</li><li>panhardware[.]com</li><li>databasegalore[.]com</li><li>freescanonline[.]com</li><li>thedoccloud[.]com</li><li>deftsecurity[.]com</li></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-40d21247e0145600a36ab00bf52f5ca4" class="builder-40d21247e0145600a36ab00bf52f5ca4 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><br/>If you have the expertise to take the additional following action items, please do so:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-c427df566506b51b60d044eae093c581" class="builder-c427df566506b51b60d044eae093c581 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>Forensically image system memory and/or host operating systems hosting all instances of SolarWinds Orion Platform [versions <strong>2019.4 HF 5</strong>, <strong>2020.2</strong> <strong>unpatched</strong>, and <strong>2020.2 HF 1</strong>].</li><li>Analyze stored network traffic for indications of compromise, including new external DNS domains.</li><li>Consult with any security vendors with whom you currently have a relationship on their recommendations.</li></ul></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-b274330da2d534dfe8e20ae7583a3142 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-b274330da2d534dfe8e20ae7583a3142" class="builder-b274330da2d534dfe8e20ae7583a3142 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;

What about my database?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-85dc7a347378b638df8f764b046cca5f {

</style><div builder-id="builder-4633527c10a413499a4ccecfec16f0cc" class="builder-4633527c10a413499a4ccecfec16f0cc builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 31</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-b24c9cc3a49d17d7455f5521b7f39f35" class="builder-b24c9cc3a49d17d7455f5521b7f39f35 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 4/6/2021</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-784f4309aa88c209099de19c62f87f19" class="builder-784f4309aa88c209099de19c62f87f19 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>If my Orion server currently or previously had a SUNBURST vulnerable version, should I simply upgrade or should I rebuild my Orion server? What about my database?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-ab452a562c9e6ded1f1dd98cf42de4d9" class="builder-ab452a562c9e6ded1f1dd98cf42de4d9 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>We encourage all customers to run only supported versions of our products and to upgrade to the latest versions to the get the full benefit of our updates, improvements and enhancements.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-1a44c03ba28563e4c6789a3320423408" class="builder-1a44c03ba28563e4c6789a3320423408 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li>If you <strong>never ran</strong> a known, affected version on your Orion server, <strong>we do not recommend that you rebuild</strong>. We still recommend as a best practice that you upgrade to the latest version, Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.5</strong>,<strong> </strong>to take advantage of the latest security improvements.</li><li>If you ran a known, affected version of your Orion server <u><strong>and</strong></u> it had access to the internet, we recommend that <strong>you rebuild the server</strong>.</li><li>If your Orion server had no access to the internet, <strong>we do not recommend that you rebuild</strong>. An upgrade to Orion Platform version <strong>2020.2.5 </strong>will be sufficient.</li></ul></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-4ecc63b74dfa5cd5374960c25ebaf34b" class="builder-4ecc63b74dfa5cd5374960c25ebaf34b builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If you rebuild your servers, here are the factors to consider in deciding whether to retain your Orion Database:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-d34adfecedc781046351d27a86cb992f" class="builder-d34adfecedc781046351d27a86cb992f builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-00eebfc6bf32c91100dd6c2863f0f427" class="builder-00eebfc6bf32c91100dd6c2863f0f427 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><ul><li><strong>Reference the </strong><a href="https://us-cert.cisa.gov/ncas/alerts/aa20-352a" dld="CISA Alert (AA20-352A) embed" dlt="Glossary Rail" target="_blank"><strong>CISA Alert (AA20-352A)</strong></a>:</li><ul><li>If you fall into <strong>Category 2</strong>, you should be able to re-install the software and retain your Orion databases for the platform, for flow, and for logs—consistent with a thorough risk evaluation.</li><li>If you fall into <strong>Category 3</strong>, your Orion installation—including the Orion databases—are equally as vulnerable as the rest of the environment, and you should follow your incident response procedures.</li></ul><li><strong>Additional considerations:</strong></li><ul><li>We are not aware of any indications at this time that the SUNBURST vulnerability routinely altered the Orion databases in any way.</li><li>You may want to audit configurations stored in the database, like alert actions, or executable scripts to confirm they have not been tampered with.</li><li>If you chose to rebuild your databases, follow your incident response procedures to image and retain your databases, particularly your flow database for forensic analysis.</li></ul></ul></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-8be129239c42a92dc84a933c3c2071c4 {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-8be129239c42a92dc84a933c3c2071c4" class="builder-8be129239c42a92dc84a933c3c2071c4 builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: stretch;

Do you know a way to do that?</h3></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-d0f2147b4180758b4f0e8c4c618a0d09 {

  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-d0f2147b4180758b4f0e8c4c618a0d09" class="builder-d0f2147b4180758b4f0e8c4c618a0d09 builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-f0c7bffccd3fe191c8a6829fba118c26" class="builder-f0c7bffccd3fe191c8a6829fba118c26 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 32</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-699387261cd6285d57b36c2c9df4cfe4" class="builder-699387261cd6285d57b36c2c9df4cfe4 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 12/16/2020</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-78256e5bdcf38bfc59ef27c3d0083685" class="builder-78256e5bdcf38bfc59ef27c3d0083685 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>I want to manually check my version of the SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll for the SUNBURST vulnerability. Do you know a way to do that?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-0fc1915736f38a0f6390f2d76b5651c4" class="builder-0fc1915736f38a0f6390f2d76b5651c4 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>You should check your Orion server, any additional polling engine, high availability (HA) engines, and additional web servers. You can use the following file hash information to check whether you have the version with the inserted vulnerability:</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div   }    </style><div builder-id="builder-f6d0106875604a641cba60217b0f892e" class="builder-f6d0106875604a641cba60217b0f892e builder-block [&amp;_img]:my-4"><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-35a20ea0aa776aff48ae2e078fb5be03" class="builder-35a20ea0aa776aff48ae2e078fb5be03 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>QUESTION 34</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-8f32887f634d58f5f5fcd58e85dea5c6" class="builder-8f32887f634d58f5f5fcd58e85dea5c6 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><em>Recent as of 12/20/2020</em></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-38046951c92a03a03effab576a7899a9" class="builder-38046951c92a03a03effab576a7899a9 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p><strong>How do I know someone didn't exploit the SUNBURST vulnerability and move horizontally in my environment and compromise another system?</strong></p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-8ee75bcdadc00f434c39ba128ea77ee8" class="builder-8ee75bcdadc00f434c39ba128ea77ee8 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>If the SUNBURST vulnerability was exploited in your environment, you may see network traffic from the Orion server to the internet. This traffic would be directed to a domain other than SolarWinds domain. Horizontal or lateral movement by an attacker would indicate that they have gained privileged level access to your environment. Increased security event monitoring, close inspection of server and application access logs, and understanding what local accounts exist on your corporate systems will help to identify any signs of misuse.</p></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">    </style><div builder-id="builder-d7a0db46790f99866e17e04db4868f89" class="builder-d7a0db46790f99866e17e04db4868f89 builder-block cs-rte-text"><div class="builder-text" style="outline:none"><p>For further details, please see <strong>CERT Alert (AA20-352A), Advanced Persistent Threat Compromise of Government Agencies, Critical Infrastructure, and Private Sector Organizations</strong>, updated December 19, 2020: <a href="https://us-cert.cisa.gov/ncas/alerts/aa20-352a" dld="https://us-cert.cisa.gov/ncas/alerts/aa20-352a embed" dlt="Glossary Rail" target="_blank">https://us-cert.cisa.gov/ncas/alerts/aa20-352a</a>.</p></div></div></div><style data-id="builderio-block" nonce="">.builder-a15bec7898e7fc4cae72e1ba44b97a7f {
    display: flex;
flex-direction: row;
align-items: center;
column-gap: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
margin-top: var(--spacing-8, 32px);
  }    </style><div builder-id="builder-a15bec7898e7fc4cae72e1ba44b97a7f" class="builder-a15bec7898e7fc4cae72e1ba44b97a7f builder-block"></div></div></div><style>.props-blocks-wrapper-b46bf284 {
  display: flex;
